name: Neck Extension
resistance_profile: Loads cervical extension; light load, slow tempo, pain-free range.
rom_notes: Lower the chin toward the chest, extend to a neutral/slightly-back position.
execution_steps: Lie face-down on a bench with your head and neck past the edge, or set up a neck harness.
Hold a light plate against the back of your head (or load the harness lightly).
Lower your chin toward your chest under control to a gentle, pain-free stretch.
Extend your head back to a neutral or slightly-back position using your neck extensors.
Pause briefly, then lower slowly to the start and repeat.
good_when: Strengthening the posterior neck for contact sports and whiplash resilience.
Balancing heavy neck flexion or shrug work for a more even neck.
You want direct, load-adjustable training of the neck extensors.
bad_when: You have any cervical pain, acute strain, or recent neck injury.
You are a novice training alone without guidance on light load and slow tempo.
Dizziness, headaches, or pinching occur at end-range extension.
